title: cephalaria mediterranea
intro: Cephalaria mediterranea, also known as Mediterranean cephalaria or goat's beard, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its tall stem and clusters of white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cephalaria mediterranea include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Cephalaria mediterranea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
